answer: Neon atoms have a stable octet. They have no tendency to share its electrons with other neon atoms.

stem: Explain why diatomic nitrogen is more stable than monoatomic nitrogen.
answer: Monoatomic nitrogen atoms are very unstable because they have five electrons in the outermost shell only. They tend to share three of its outermost shell electrons with another nitrogen atom, forming a triple covalent bond. In this way, each nitrogen atom has a stable octet.

stem: What is the name given to Group VII elements of the periodic table?
Explain why elements in Group VII exist as diatomic molecules.
answer: An atom of a Group VII element has 7 outermost shell electrons. Each Group VII atom can obtain the stable electronic arrangement of an atom of a noble gas by sharing one outermost shell electron with another Group VII atom.

stem: What is the number of neighbouring chloride ions directly attached to each sodium ion in crystalline sodium chloride?

stem: Explain how the particles are held together in solid sodium metal.
answer: The metal ions and delocalized electrons in solid sodium metal are held together by metallic bonds

stem: Explain how the particles are held together in solid sodium chloride.
answer: When force is applied, layers of ions can slide over one another. Non-directional metallic bonds continue to hold the metal ions and therefore the sodium metal does not break
